judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports are strong and nearly identical on points 1-3, but Report 2 additionally identifies the missing environment-level master switch and explicitly recommends adding one ANDed with the per-tenant flag (expectation d), plus catches a UI/backend default mismatch bug that Report 1 misses, making Report 2 the more complete answer.

report_1.evidence: Reminders **are sent automatically**. The decision is made in `auto_send_reminders_enabled()` at `slotwise/backend/app/feature_flags.py:10-13`... Line 11-12 is the crux: if the key is absent (`.get()` returns `None`), it returns `True`.

report_2.evidence: Reminders **are sent automatically**. The decision lives in `auto_send_reminders_enabled` at `backend/app/feature_flags.py:10-13`... Line 11-12: when the key is missing from `tenant.settings` (i.e. `None`), the function returns `True`.

report_1.evidence: I would change the default to `False` (fail closed) at `slotwise/backend/app/feature_flags.py:11-12`, changing `return True` to `return False` for `auto_send_reminders_enabled`, so a clinic must explicitly opt in via the settings UI

report_2.evidence: I would change `backend/app/feature_flags.py:11-12` to invert the null case to `False`

report_1.evidence: Defaulting to "on" is defensible for `compact_calendar_view_enabled` (`feature_flags.py:4-7`) — it's a cosmetic UI preference with no external side effects and no harm if wrong. But `auto_send_reminders_enabled` (`feature_flags.py:10-13`) gates outbound SMS/email to patients — an external-facing communication with real-world consequences... Per `git show aa4cf8d` (SLOT-214), `auto_send_reminders_enabled` was added as a copy-paste of the pattern already established by `compact_calendar_view_enabled`

report_2.evidence: That's a meaningfully different risk profile than `compact_calendar_view_enabled`, which is a pure display preference where defaulting on is low-cost if wrong... `git show 25509a7` (SLOT-190, 2026-06-10) added `compact_calendar_view_enabled` alone, a legitimate default-on convenience toggle. `git show aa4cf8d` (SLOT-214, 2026-07-01) then added `auto_send_reminders_enabled` directly beneath it, with the identical `if ... is None: return True` structure

Confirm there is no env-level master switch anywhere — I found none in `infra/main.tf` (only an `environment` variable) or elsewhere in this repo. Per project documentation's flag recipe, add an env-level master gate ANDed with the per-tenant value, and document a rollback affordance (e.g., an env var that force-disables the job) before this goes live
